Therrin Protze Chief Operating Officer Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex, operated Delaware North.
named Visitor Complex January 2014 made enhancing guest experience priority since began.
Protze oversees aspects Delaware North's operation Visitor Complex, which done behalf NASA since 1995.
popular Florida destination, only minutes east Orlando, welcomes visitors come tour Kennedy Space Center, experience such attractions Space Shuttle Atlantis®, Shuttle Launch Experience® even live rockets launches.
Since being named COO, Protze spearheaded major upgrades additions enhance guest experience while telling NASA's inspirational story.
Major projects include Astronaut Training Experience® Heroes Legends featuring U.S. Astronaut Hall Fame®.
Future projects include series improvements enhancements prepare return crewed launches from Kennedy Space Center.
addition, Delaware North awarded early extension from NASA continue operating Visitor Complex until 2028 achieved record attendance 2016 2017.
joined Delaware North 2006 Vice President Gaming Operations global headquarters Buffalo, where supervised operational performance over three casinos.
Before joining Delaware North, Protze worked with Harrah's, Trump Barden Companies senior director operations Majestic Star Casino.
Protze spent eight years United States Marine Corps prior time gaming industry.
While Marine Corps, Protze earned rank sergeant held several leadership responsibilities.
every place Protze worked lived, he's always served supported community organizations.
participates five community boards, under leadership, Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex participated consecutive record-breaking United campaigns, raising millions dollars community.
2016, Therrin received Distinguished Public Service Medal awarded NASA.
